title: crepis praemorsa subsp. caucasigena
intro: Crepis praemorsa subsp. caucasigena, also known as Caucasian hawk's beard or Caucasus hawk's beard, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae), native to the Caucasus region. Traditionally, Crepis praemorsa subsp. caucasigena has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Crepis praemorsa subsp. caucasigena can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
